config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Society matters, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because that easily becomes an excuse for abuse by governments or majorities. In my view, a healthy society protects people first, then asks individuals to contribute responsibly within clear limits. I believe the burden should be on society to justify any restriction of rights, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kita justru penuh contoh bahwa mayoritas dan pemerintah bisa salah, jadi hak individu perlu menjadi batas yang kuat terhadap kekuasaan, bukan sesuatu yang mudah dikorbankan. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi pembatasan hak hanya layak jika benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, “demi masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still reject the idea that society should automatically come first over the individual. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how appeals to “public interest” can be used to silence criticism, pressure minorities, or excuse weak accountability, so I do not trust that phrase unless it is tightly limited. Society does matter, but it is strongest when individual rights are treated as real protections, not as privileges that can be traded away whenever the majority feels inconvenienced. The better principle is balance: protect rights first, and only restrict them when there is a clear, necessary, and fair reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: hak individu tidak boleh ditempatkan di bawah kepentingan masyarakat secara otomatis. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, justru pengalaman politik kami menunjukkan bahwa mayoritas dan pemerintah bisa salah, dan ketika “demi masyarakat” dijadikan mantra, itu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dibuktikan dengan alasan yang jelas, perlu, dan proporsional, bukan diasumsikan lebih tinggi dari hak dasar. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang paling disiplin melindungi kebebasan sambil membatasi kekuasaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over individual rights. From my Indonesian perspective, I have seen how “for the public good” can become a convenient excuse for silencing criticism, protecting officials, or pressuring minorities, so I do not trust that phrase unless it is tightly constrained. A society is healthier when rights are treated as real limits on power, not as flexible privileges that can be waived whenever the majority wants convenience. Public interests matter, but they should justify restrictions only when they are necessary, proportionate, and truly accountable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa sering “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan kekuasaan yang berlebihan, dan sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa mayoritas maupun pemerintah bisa keliru dengan cara yang serius. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi justru masyarakat yang paling sehat adalah masyarakat yang membatasi diri untuk melindungi kebebasan dasar, bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan individu. Kalau ada pembatasan hak, beban pembuktiannya harus sangat kuat: perlu, proporsional, dan benar-benar dapat dipertanggungjawabkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
